message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="9/9 consensus! Mini-retro then Step 8">
9/9 consensus achieved on Step 7 (Clarify as Project)! 

Please hold your mini-retro, then plan Step 8: Waiting For. Carson — update docs/gtd-product-discovery.md (mark Step 7 [x], move NEXT to Step 8, update current step). The team should also discuss driver rotation for Step 8.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Acknowledged. Time for the mini-retro, then Step 8 planning. Let me kick off the mini-retro discussion with the team.

input.content:
Congrats on 9/9 consensus for Step 7! Before you update the docs, let's do the mini-retro (pipeline step 12). Three questions for the team:

(a) **Did we follow the pipeline?** From my perspective, yes -- TDD, fmt, clippy, test, refactor, glossary check, commit, consensus, push, CI green.

(b) **Was the commit atomic?** Yes -- the clarify-as-project workflow was a single cohesive change: service function, route handler update, template additions, tests, glossary updates.

(c) **Any process improvements?** One observation: the validation error dead-end that Steve Krug flagged could have been caught earlier if we had a checklist item in the Definition of Done specifically about error recovery paths (every error state must have a way back to a working state). This was caught in review, which is the right time, but worth noting.

Also, the coordinator mentioned we should discuss driver rotation for Step 8 (Waiting For). Step 8 involves a new domain type (WaitingForItem), new DB table, new service, new routes, and new UI. Thoughts on who should drive? The pattern is similar to what we've built for Next Actions and Projects -- I'd be comfortable driving the Rust backend, but Carson has been doing excellent work driving the full-stack implementation. Open to the team's view.
input.summary: Mini-retro kickoff and Step 8 driver discussion

routing.summary: Mini-retro kickoff and Step 8 driver discussion

(a) **Did we follow the pipeline?** From my perspective, yes -- TDD, fmt, clippy, test, refactor, glossary check, commit, consensus, push, CI green.

(b) **Was the commit atomic?** Yes -- the clarify-as-project workflow was a single cohesive change: service function, route handler update, template additions, tests, glossary updates.

(c) **Any process improvements?** One observation: the validation error dead-end that Steve Krug flagged could have been caught earlier if we had a checklist item in the Definition of Done specifically about error recovery paths (every error state must have a way back to a working state). This was caught in review, which is the right time, but worth noting.

Also, the coordinator mentioned we should discuss driver rotation for Step 8 (Waiting For). Step 8 involves a new domain type (WaitingForItem), new DB table, new service, new routes, and new UI. Thoughts on who should drive? content.text: Mini-retro kicked off with Carson. I've shared my observations on all three checkpoint questions and opened the driver rotation discussion for Step 8. Standing by for the team's responses.
